original: Start of the Lunar Cycle of Hilarius
Islamic Era begins: Muhammad and his followers begin migration from Mecca to Medina (Hijra)

The Great Schism between Western and Eastern churches begins when Roman Cardinal Humbert issues a bull of excommunication against Cerularius, Patriarch of Constantinople, on the altar of the Hagia Sophia in Constantinople
Crusaders herd Jews of Jerusalem into a synagogue & set it afire
Battle of Las Navas de Tolosa [Battle of Al-Uqab]: combined Christian army defeats Almohad Muslim force in a turning point for Muslim power on Iberian peninsula
The Virgin Mary gives Simon Stock a Brown Scapular (legend)
The six Electors of the Holy Roman Empire signed the Agreement of Rhense confirming Emperor Louis IV.
Kissing is banned in England to stop the spread of the Black Death
La Paz, Bolivia, is founded
Alva demands submission of Zealand and Holland
Nighttime naval battle off the coast of Peru between a Dutch fleet led by Joris Spilbergen and a Spanish fleet carrying silver; two Spanish ships sink
Captain John Gilbert patents first dredger in Britain
William IIIbecomes stadtholder of Zeeland
Manchu/Chinese Qing Dynasty naval forces under commander Shi Lang defeat the Kingdom of Tungning in the Battle of Penghu near the Pescadores Islands.
Turkish troops under Kara Mustafa reach Vienna
Congress declares the city of Washington in the District of Columbia, the permanent capital of the United States
US Public Health Service forms, and US Marine Hospital is authorized
La Paz, Bolivia, declares its independence from the Spanish Crown and forms the Junta Tuitiva led by Pedro Domingo Murillo, the first independent government in Spanish America
Statue of Laurens Jansz Coster unveiled in Haarlem
Sir Henry Havelock arrives at the Battle of Cawnpore
Manassas Campaign [->July 22]
Utrecht-Swells railway opens
Amstel Hotel, "the dignified old lady," opens in Amsterdam
D.R. Averill of Ohio patents ready-mixed paint
Joseph Monier patents reinforced concrete
Dr. Emily Stowe becomes the first woman licenced to practise medicine in Canada.
Many African American miners in Alabama killed by striking white miners
Treaty of Aoki-Kimberley signed between Japan and England
Lancashire batsman Archie MacLaren scores the first quadruple-hundred (424) in first-class cricket against Somerset at Taunton
Indian-born K. S. Ranjitsinhji debuts for England against Australia in the second Test at Old Trafford; he is the first Indian to play Test cricket
A report appears in London that all foreigners in Peking, China, have been massacred. Although soon exposed as false, the report helps mobilize support for relief of foreigners
Russia launch an offensive against the Chinese in Manchuria
The Fawcett Commission headed by Millicent Fawcett is established as a result of an outcry against the treatment of Afrikaners in concentration camps during the South African War
John McGraw officially becomes manager of MLB New York Giants; 30-year tenure begins (1902-32)
Islands of Manu'a group (Samoa) ceded to US by their chiefs
MLB Detroit Tigers and Washington Senators play longest scoreless game in AL history - 18 innings at Bennett Field in Detroit, Michigan
Naval torpedo launched from an airplane patented by B. A. Fiske
Socialist conference in Brussels (Kautsky, Trotski, and Rosa Luxemburg)
General Amos Fries is appointed as the first U.S. Army Chemical Warfare Service Chief
Conference over German recovery payments begins in London
New York Giants' George Kelly is the first to hit home runs in six consecutive games
The first parliament in Iraq opened by King Feisal in Baghdad
Jaspar government asks authority to save Belgian franc
National Geographic takes first natural-color undersea photos
Augusto César Sandino begins 5½-year war against US occupation of Nicaragua
First automatic parking meter in the United States is installed in Oklahoma City, Oklahoma
First X-ray photo of arterial circulation in Rochester, NY
The New York Giants are 10½ games back in the NL and go on to win the pennant
NSB'er Woudenberg is appointed as NVV trustee
100°F (38°C) highest temperature ever recorded in Seattle, Washington
Joe DiMaggiogoes 3 for 4, hitting in his 56th consecutive game
Police take Jewish citizens (2,573 men; 5,165 women; and 3,625 children) from their homes in Paris and incarcerate them at the Vélodrome d'Hiver stadium to process them for deportation
Dodgers score 8 unearned runs against Braves to win 8-5 and break their 16-game losing streak; they will lose another 5 in a row
Australian Services win third Victory Test Cricket by four wickets
Cruiser Indianapolis leaves San Francisco with atomic bomb
A US court martial sentences 46 members of the SS to death for Battle of the Bulge crimes in Dachau
Attempt made to recall Mayor Lapham (first time in San Francisco history)
Bobo Newsom wins his 200th game, his first as a Yankee, and the Yankees' 18th straight in the nightcap Vic Raschi extends the streak to 19
FIFA World Cup Final, Estádio do Maracanã, Rio de Janeiro, Brazil: Alcides Ghiggia scores a 79th minute winner as Uruguay defeats Brazil 2-1
Single-day 16-team home run record set at 37 (NL-25, AL-12)
Len Hutton scores his 100th century, Yorkshire vs. Surrey at The Oval
Leopold III, King of the Belgians, abdicates in favor of his son Baudouin I
KROC (now KTTC) TV channel 10 in Rochester, Minnesota (NBC) broadcasts for the first time
Detroit Tigers and Briggs Stadium are sold for a then-record $5.5 million
Karelo-Finnish SSR becomes part of Russian SFSR
King Faisal of Iraq begins visit to England
Last Ringling Bros. and Barnum & Bailey Circus under a canvas tent
205,000 (record sports attend) see Brazil-Uruguay World Cup soccer
George Crowe sets record of 12 pinch hit HRs with a runner on
Amazon river carries 190,000 m3/sec of water (record)
Republican convention selects Barry Goldwater as presidential candidate
Mont Blanc Road tunnel between France & Italy opens
Nigeria becomes the first Anglophone independent state in Africa to become an associate member of European Economic Community
Number of banned persons in South Africa totals 936; the individuals are banned under various laws, most prominently the Suppression of Communism and Riotous Assembly Acts
American pop-rock group "The Monkees" perform at Forest Hills Tennis Stadium in Queens, New York, with psychedelic rock Jimi Hendrix Experience as opening act;
British rock band "The Who" begin 1st full US tour, appearing at the Fillmore Auditorium in San Francisco, California, and then going coast-to-coast opening for Herman's Hermits
Prison brawl ignites barracks in Jay, Florida, killing 37
Minnesota Twins'Rod Carewsteals home for his career best 7th time of the season, against Chicago
9th British Commonwealth Games open in Edinburgh, Scotland
Cincinnati Reds spoil Pirates debut in Pittsburgh's new Three Rivers Stadium, 3-2
Iraq's constitution goes into effect
The Social Democratic and Labour Party (SDLP) withdraw from Stormont (North Ireland Parliament) after no inquiry is announced into the shooting dead of Seamus Cusack and Desmond Beattie
Dimitrios Papadopoulos becomes 269th patriarch of Constantinople
During Watergate hearings, Butterfield reveals existence of tapes
Rock duo Loggins & Messina break up after 6 years
Polish railway workers block railway to Russia
India performs nuclear Test
Shukuni Sasaki spins 72 plates simultaneously
CDC report on AIDS symptoms in three hemophiliacs
NASA launches Landsat 4 to create thematic maps of Earth
Sikorsky S-61 disaster: A helicopter crashes off the Isles of Scilly, causing 20 fatalities in Britain's worst helicopter accident
Bill to abolish Greater London Council receives royal assent
Great Britain performs nuclear Test at Nevada Test Site
New York YankeesDon Mattinglyhits his 4th grand slam of season & ties AL record of homers in 6 straight games (on way to tie major league record of 8)
Said Aouita runs world record 2000m (4:50.81)
Carl Lewisruns a wind-assisted 100m in 9.78 sec
Florence Joyner runs 100m in women's world record 10.49 seconds
Landmark Japanese animated cyberpunk manga film "Akira" released, directed by Katsuhiro Otomo
San Antonio (Texas League) beats Jackson 1-0 in 26 innings
South Africa's largest labour federation, the Congress of South African Trade Unions, holds its third annual congress and intensifies its campaign against apartheid
400 die in a (7.7) earthquake in the Philippines
Civil trial by parents of Suicide victims against Judas Priest begins
NYC's Empire State Building catches fire-no fatalities
Parliament of Ukraine adopts Declaration of State Sovereignty , laying the groundwork for independence from the Soviet Union
Rick Dee's "Into the Night" premieres on ABC-TV
President Lissouba calls emergency rule in Congo-Brazzaville
S. van Ruysdael's "Winter Landscape" sold for £705,500 in London
SF outfielder Darren Lewis sets record of 267 consecutive errorless games
Baseball Night in America premieres (no Saturday day games)
Comet Shoemaker-Levy first collides with Jupiter (collisions continue until 22 July)
Shreveport Pirates lose first CFL home game 35-34 to the Toronto Argonauts at Independence Stadium in Shreveport, Louisiana
Spanish fishing boats sink a French fishing boat over fishing rights
14th ESPY Awards:Lance Armstrong, Annika Sörenstam win

An earthquake of magnitude 6.8 and aftershock of 6.6 occurs off the Niigata coast, Japan, killing 8 people with at least 800 injured and damaging a nuclear power plant. See 2007 Chūetsu offshore earthquake.
Malaysian politician Anwar Ibrahim is arrested on sodomy charges in Malaysia
NASA's Dawn space probe enters orbit around the protoplanet Vesta
27 children are killed and 25 are hospitalized after eating lunch contaminated with insecticide in India
Death toll within Gaza surpasses 200 people following air strikes by Israel
Hamas and Islamic Jihad offer Israel a 10-year truce with 10 conditions, which include lifting the blockade and the release of prisoners; it is rejected
Israel intensifies strikes on Gaza
Scientists reveal the first close-up pictures of Pluto sent by the New Horizons probe
Shootings in Chattanooga at a US military recruitment centre and a naval reserve training center kill five and injure others
BBC announces first-ever female Doctor in "Doctor Who" will be played by Jodie Whittaker
12 new moons discovered orbiting Jupiter bringing planet's moon total to 79, by scientists at Carnegie Institution for Science
Historic cemetery (1878-1911) announced discovered near Houston, Texas, containing 95 remains though to be African Americans forced into labour
Highest temperature ever recorded in China of 52.2 C (126 F) at Sanbao township in Xinjiang's Turpan Depression
Jam rock band Dead & Company, an outgrowth of the Grateful Dead, plays the final show of its final concert tour, at Oracle Park in San Francisco, California
Persian Gulf International Airport on Iran’s southwestern coast  hits a heat index high of 152 degrees Fahrenheit (66.7 C) at 12:30 p.m., beyond what humans can endure
Paraguay carries out the largest cocaine bust in its history with "Operation Sweetness" netting four tons inside a shipment of sugar
At least 61 people die in fire at the recently opened five-story Al-Kornish Hypermarket and restaurant in Kut, Iraq
At least 63 people die in monsoon torrential rains in Punjab, Pakistan, within 24 hours

Members of rock band Jane’s Addiction file dueling lawsuits over singer Perry Farrell's onstage scuffle with guitarist Dave Navarro in Boston, which resulted in cancellation of their reunion tour and a planned album
One of three known "Rosebud" props from the 1941 film 'Citizen Kane' sells at auction for $14.75M
The Israeli Air Force bombs the Syrian Defense Ministry headquarters and areas near the presidential palace in Damascus
Richard II, aged 10, crowned King of England at Westminster Abbey, succeeding his grandfatherEdward III
Joan of Arcand the French army enter the city of Rheims
Battle at Montlhéry betweenLouis XIand the League of the Public Weal
John Adamsgraduates from Harvard
Father Junipero Serra founds Mission San Diego the 1st mission in California
Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart's opera "Die Entführung aus dem Serail (The Abduction from the Seraglio)" premieres in Vienna withMozartconducting
French KingLouis XVIreinstatesJacques Neckeras finance minister following riots at his dismissal
Pope Pius VIIandNapoleonsign  the Concordat of 1801, guaranteeing religious freedom for Catholics living in France
David Farragutbecomes the first Rear Admiral in the US Navy

The South African Committee, investigating the Jameson Raid releases its report finding that it was conducted almost implicitly through the support and encouragement ofCecil Rhodesand the mining houses in the Transvaal
Encouraged by the British, King Constantine of Greece launches a campaign to take Asia Minor from nationalists underMustafa Kemal Atatürk
Adolf Hitlerorders preparations for the invasion of Britain (Operation Sealion)
MLB manager merry-go-round: Philadelphia Phillies - Ben Chapman out, Eddie Sawyer in; NY Giants -Mel Ottout,Leo Durocherin; Brooklyn Dodgers - Durocher out, Burt Shotton in
Novel "Catcher in Rye" byJ. D. Salingeris published by Little Brown and Company
Juan Manuel Fangioof Argentina clinches his third Formula 1 World Drivers' Championship by finishing second to fellow Mercedes driverStirling Mossin the British Grand Prix at Aintree
US Marine MajorJohn Glennsets transcontinental speed record (03:28:08)
Ralph Bostonof US, sets then long jump record at 27' 2"
72 year-oldMao Zedongswims the Yangtze River near Wuhan to prove his strength as part of his launch of the Great Proletarian Cultural Revolution
Canadian singer-songwriterJoni Mitchellmakes her Newport Folk Festival debut at a songwriters' workshop performance in Newport, Rhode Island
Smokey Robinsonand the Miracles final live performance, in Washington D.C.
Major League Baseball owners re-elect CommissionerBowie Kuhnto a 7-year term
Iraqi President Ahmed Hassan al-Bakr is succeeded bySaddam Hussein
Ronald Reagannominated for US President by Republicans in Detroit
George P. Shultzis sworn in as the 60th US Secretary of State
Sun Myung Moonsentenced to 18 months for tax fraud
Jackie Joyner-Kerseesets women's heptathlete record of 7,215 pts
The ANC send a report on police violence to PresidentF. W. de Klerkand demand an end to "the shocking inhumanity" of police action in rural areas of South Africa

"The Three Tenors"Plácido Domingo,José Carreras, andLuciano Pavarotti, perform with the Los Angeles Philharmonic and chorus of the Los Angeles Opera, conducted by Zubin Mehta at Dodger Stadium in Los Angeles, California; an estimated 1.3 billion viewers watch the worldwide broadcast

John F. Kennedy Jr., his wife Carolyn Bessette Kennedy and sister-in-law Lauren Bessette are killed in a plane crash off the coast of Martha's Vineyard; the Piper Saratoga aircraft was piloted by Kennedy
"Jurassic Park III", starringSam NeillandWilliam H. Macy, premieres in the US
11th ESPY Awards:Lance Armstrong,Serena Williamswin
Martha Stewartis sentenced to five months in prison plus five months in home confinement for lying to federal investigators
Millennium Park, considered the first and most ambitious architectural project in the early 21st century for Chicago, is opened to the public by MayorRichard M. Daley.
"Harry Potter and the Half-Blood Prince", the 6th book in the series byJ. K. Rowling, is published worldwide. 9 million copies sell in 24 hrs
"The Dark Knight Rises", directed byChristopher Nolan, starringChristian BaleandAnne Hathaway, premieres in New York
Bashar al-Assadis sworn in for a third term as President of Syria
Republican presidential nomineeDonald Trumpannounces Indiana governorMike Penceas his Vice Presidential running mate
Kawhi Leonardre-signs with the San Antonio Spurs to a five-year, $90 million contract
Wimbledon Men's Tennis:Roger Federerbeats Marin Čilić 6-3, 6-1, 6-4 for a record 8th Wimbledon men's title
Musical film sequel "Mamma Mia! - Here We Go Again" premieres at Hammersmith-Apollo, London; worldwide box office receipts total over $400 million

US PresidentDonald Trumpappears to accept the word of Russian PresidentVladimir Putinover US intelligence services about Russian meddling in the 2016 US election, in an interview after the two leader's Helsinki Summit
Rev.Jesse Jacksonsteps down as head of Rainbow/PUSH after leading the non-profit organization for more than five decades
Taylor Swift’s re-recorded "Speak Now" album debuts at No. 1 on Billboard 200, her 12th No. 1 album, surpassing Barbra Streisand's record for #1 albums by a female artist
Public debate betweenMartin Lutherand theologian Johann Eck at Pleissenburg Castle in Liepzig, during which Luther denies the divine right of the Pope
First banknotes in Europe are issued by the Bank of Stockholm
Fabian Gottlieb von Bellingshausensets sail to explore Antarctica for Tsar Alexander I
First test detonation of an atomic bomb occurs at Trinity Site, Alamogordo, New Mexico, as part of the US Manhattan Project
Apollo 11 launches, carrying the first men to land on the Moon
